metadata.title:
FRED MILLER, SPOKANE AT.
TORNEY FOR FEDERATION,
NOT SURPRISED AT MISSING
MANS APPEARANCE IN SPO-
KANE.

pages.raw_text:
BOISE, Oct. 16.-A dispatch
from Spokane that Jack Simpkins
had been seen and recognized by a
friend there, caused no surprise
here with those connected with the
defense Of Western Federation
men.


Fred Miller, the Spokane attor-
ney, stated that Simpkins was
much changed in appearance. that
he had shaved his mustache, and
would not be recognized by any but
those very we'll acquainted WIZ
him.


While in Spokane Saturday
Simpkins talked freely with a
Press reporter who is acquainted
with him. He said he had no fear
Of the regular authorities, as there
was no warrant out for him. but did
not want to fall into the hands of
the Pinkertons, believing from
their treatment of other miners
union officials that they would oe
able to hold him in prison on
trumped up charges for an indefin-
lite period. He was of the opinion
that there is no longer any strong
desire to apprehend him, but de.
clined TO take chances.
